findings.title: Turn completion mentions are snapshotted before late participants arrive
findings.description: `process_turn_events` builds `mentions` once, at turn start. Mid-turn inject paths later add users into `turn_participants`, but the final success/error notifications still use the stale snapshot. In practice, anyone who joins after the turn begins will be omitted from that turn's completion/error ping, so the new per-turn participant tracking is already out of date before it is used.
findings.suggestion: Read `turn_participants` immediately before sending each completion/error notification, or otherwise keep a live per-turn mention list instead of snapshotting it up front.

findings.title: Permission prompts can be attributed to the wrong user
findings.description: The new `current_triggered_by` is a single mutable session-wide variable that is overwritten whenever another queued or injected message is consumed, then later copied into `PermissionRequest`. If one message starts work, a second message is injected, and only then a permission request is emitted for the first message's tool call, the UI will ping the second user instead of the one who actually triggered the action. The same race exists in the background-task paths because they also reuse this shared mutable value.
findings.suggestion: Capture `triggered_by` in the specific turn/inject context that spawned the tool-producing work and thread that value through to the eventual `PermissionRequest`, instead of reading a session-global mutable field when the permission event is emitted.

findings.title: Manual session teardown leaves stale `turn_participants` entries behind
findings.description: The diff adds `turn_participants` cleanup for eviction and TTL sweeping, but explicit teardown paths still do not remove the per-thread `HashSet<UserId>`. `/session del` only clears `session_skills`, `permission_rxs`, and `needs_context`, and the unregister loop has the same omission. That leaves stale participant sets attached to dead sessions until some later sweep and defeats the intended cleanup for this new map.
findings.suggestion: Remove the thread id from `turn_participants` everywhere a session is explicitly killed or deleted, including `/session del`, unregister, and any similar teardown paths.

summary: Field additions are propagated through the touched constructors, but the diff introduces two per-turn attribution races and misses cleanup for `turn_participants` on explicit session teardown paths.
